data.title: Robinhood Markets Stock (HOOD) Sinks 9% as Trading Volumes Decline
data.description:
The stock of Robinhood Markets (HOOD) is down 9% on Dec. 11 after the online brokerage reported a sharp drop in its November trading volumes.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Robinhood, whose trading platform and app are popular with retail investors, reported that its equity trading volumes fell 37% month-over-month during November. Additionally, options volumes declined 28%, and cryptocurrency trading volumes fell 12%.

With both equity and crypto markets weakening during November, assets under custody at Robinhood dropped 5% month-over-month to $325 billion, the first monthly decline since February of this year. The trading platform also said that its funded accounts declined by 130,000.

Robinhood's Net Deposits Rise

Despite the soft trading volumes, November delivered Robinhood's third-best month of the year for net deposits at $7.1 billion, up nearly 27% from October. Earlier this week, Robinhood Markets announced its entry into Indonesia.

Robinhood's predictions market was a standout growth area for the company in November, with three billion event contracts traded, up 20% from October and 500% year-over-year. Robinhood is holding a predictions market event on Dec. 16, which some analysts say could be the next catalyst to lift HOOD stock.

Is HOOD Stock a Buy?

Robinhood's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 20 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 14 Buy, five Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average HOOD price target of $151.39 implies 22.57% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: 'It's Still Rock-Solid,' Says Bank of America About Oracle Stock
data.description:
Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) stock is feeling the heat on Thursday, tumbling 10% after its fiscal Q2 results failed to win over investors. And if you were among those who rushed to hit the sell button, Bank of America thinks you may have jumped the gun. But before we get to their case, let's break down what caused the sell-off in the first place.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

The company just missed on the topline, delivering revenue of $16.06 billion, amounting to a 13.9% year-over-year increase yet falling shy of the consensus estimate by $130 million. On the other hand, adj. EPS came in at $2.26, well above analyst expectations of $1.64. That said, the outperformance was largely driven by the sale of its Ampere chip business to SoftBank, which added $0.91 per diluted share to pretax earnings.

But the disappointment seemed to lie elsewhere. For one, Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) revenue increased 69% year-over-year, hitting market expectations of around $4.1 billion but failing to outperform. Moreover, capital expenditures came in at $12 billion, $3.8 billion above consensus, resulting in negative free cash flow of nearly $10 billion for the quarter. During the conference call, management noted that FY26 capital spending (ending May 31) is expected to be $15 billion higher than previously anticipated.

With the company now entering into the "heaviest phase of its AI infrastructure buildout," Bank of America analyst Brad Sills argues that the quarter didn't signal a deterioration in Oracle's business, but rather exposed the "timing mismatch of buildout spend to revenue conversion." Sills pointed to the underwhelming OCI revenue growth and the elevated capex required as Oracle ramps its infrastructure to support the massive $523 billion OCI AI backlog. Still, Sills frames the shortfall as an "investment curve issue rather than a change in fundamentals."

And that's where his thesis lands. Fundamentals "remain strong," he says, and the present weakness is down to elevated capex needed to keep pace with rapid AI demand. Underlying trends are positive: AI demand "continues to swell," large site builds are on schedule, OCI's "fungible architecture" supports diverse compute needs, and the company maintains strong financing access while upholding investment-grade credit.

As for the stock, it has now given back all of its gains "and some" since the September OpenAI announcement, reflecting "skittish investor confidence in Oracle's ability to transform into an AI compute hyperscaler." Yet, Sills thinks there is room for sentiment and estimates to move higher. AI spending remains strong across a wide range of enterprises, and Oracle's applications business is showing "early signs of an AI halo effect," with revenue accelerating to 11%, up 200bps year-over-year.

"We therefore focus on the catalysts that could drive upward stock momentum, namely accelerating revenue conversion, improving visibility into the OCI AI margin profile, and AI-driven momentum across applications and database offerings," the analyst summed up.

Bottom line, Sills assigns ORCL a Buy rating, but given "valuation contraction across the AI cohort," his price objective goes from $368 to $300. Nevertheless, the new target still makes room for 12-month returns of 50%. (To watch Sills' track record, click here)

22 other analysts also take a positive view of Oracle's prospects and with an additional 11 Holds and 1 Sell, the analyst consensus rates the shares a Moderate Buy. There are plenty of gains projected here now; at $346.11, the average price target implies the stock will climb 73% higher over the one-year timeframe. (See ORCL stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Government Shutdown Odds Surge after Senate Rejects ACA Subsidies Extension
data.description:
The odds of the government shutting down again by January 31, 2026, surged to 48% on prediction platform Kalshi after the Senate failed to pass a Democratic bill that would extend Affordable Care Act (ACA) healthcare subsidies for three years. The odds have risen by 8% during the past 24 hours and 17% during the past week.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Extending the subsidies was a major point of contention between the two parties that led to the previous shutdown, which ended on November 12 after lasting for a record 43 days.

ACA Subsidies Extension Bill Falls Short Despite Bipartisan Support

The Senate consists of 53 Republicans and 47 Democrats, and requires 60 votes for a proposal to pass. The ACA extension proposal received 51 "yes" votes, with all Democrats and four Republicans voting in favor. Senate Republicans proposed an alternative bill that would lead to some Americans receiving federal assistance to help pay out-of-pocket healthcare costs, although the measure failed to pass as well with zero Democratic votes.

The ACA open enrollment period for plans that begin on January 1, 2026, is set to close on December 15.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Dives as Concerns About Missile Chips, Deals Mount
data.description:
Chip stock Intel (INTC) has had its share of problems lately, but one new one is a potentially more serious problem than anything seen yet. Intel is currently being sued in a Texas state court over the possibility that its chips are currently in Russian missiles being fired into Ukraine. This development has investors pretty rattled, as demonstrated by the 3% loss in its share price seen in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

The suit alleges that Intel, among others, failed to keep certain technologies out of Russian-made weapons that went on to both kill and injure civilians in Ukraine. It also accuses Intel of "willful ignorance," with third parties selling restricted chips that were used to drive both drones and missiles. It cites five separate incidents, including one that ended in the death of dozens.

The law firm Baker & Hostetler was connected to this suit, filed on behalf of "…dozens of Ukrainian civilians." Intel did not immediately comment, though earlier comment in a similar vein notes that Intel is in compliance with all sanction requirements and left Russia altogether when the war first tstarted.

Conflict of Interest?

Meanwhile, concerns are also mounting over the SambaNova Systems deal Intel is trying to work out. Perhaps the biggest concern is connected to Lip-Bu Tan, who serves as CEO of Intel and executive chairman of SambaNova.

These concerns may be largely moot, though. Intel's board already requires Tan to recuse himself during any investment choice where he could gain, leaving such matters to chief financial officer David Zinsner as well as the rest of the board. Intel also has a special audit committee that is tasked with considering such deals in light of established policy.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on five Buys, 24 Holds and six Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 96.25% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $37.04 per share implies 6.01% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Gains as Evio Reveals New Hybrid-Electric Aircraft
data.description:
Trying to introduce alternate-fuel aircraft has been a bit of a challenge over the last few years. But aerospace stock Boeing (BA) recently put some support behind Evio, a company that may have brought out a real winner in the field. Evio introduced a new hybrid-electric airliner geared toward regional travel. That news was good enough for Boeing investors, who gave Boeing stock nearly a 1.5% boost in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Evio rolled out the Evio 810, a "clean-sheet design" aircraft model that it hopes to have available for purchase starting in the 2030s, though hopefully, in the early 2030s. Interestingly, Evio already has orders for 450 of the 810 so far, including from two "major" airlines who remain unnamed looking to buy 250 of the aircraft. Those airlines also have the option to buy another 200 down the line.

The airliner offers seats for 76 passengers, and Evio believes that the 810 can have applications in both cargo carrying and defense as well. Range and similar specifications have not yet been revealed. The company's CEO, Michael Derman, noted, "From day one, our focus has been on increasing profitability for regional operators and providing an exceptional passenger experience. We've recognized early on that a strong-hybrid architecture can provide unmatched efficiencies for airlines, helping them sustain vibrant regional networks in a cost-effective, responsible way."

New Comms Test

Meanwhile, a new team-up of Boeing and United Airlines (UAL) is working together on a new kind of data communication technology that could improve information flow between pilots in the air and air traffic control on the ground. The system depends on Internet Protocol Suite (IPS) technology, and may one day represent a fundamental change in how air traffic works.

The system was installed aboard a 737-8 United owned, which went by the name of the 2025 Boeing ecoDemonstrator Explorer. Said plane ran a series of missions from Houston, as well as a set from Edinburgh, to demonstrate how the aircraft communications system would work. Reports suggest the tests went well, but a full rollout to the broader industry was likely to take years to accomplish.

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 13 Buys, two Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After an 18.46% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $248.77 per share implies 23.48% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Crypto Exchange Gemini's Stock (GEMI) Is Up 25% on News It's Launching a Predictions Market
data.description:
Shares of Gemini (GEMI) are up 25% on news that the crypto exchange is launching a predictions market.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Gemini, which was founded by twin brothers Cameron and Tyler Winklevoss, has received a designated contract market license, or DCM, from the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), that enables the company to offer users a predictions market on its website and mobile app.

Prediction markets like Polymarket allow users to trade CFTC-regulated event contracts on yes-or-no questions. Traders can wager on everything from the outcome of football games and elections to the color of U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent's necktie on a given day.

The Growth of Betting

Gemini first applied for a DCM license in 2020 but was denied. The company thanked the CFTC's more relaxed approach to cryptocurrencies and financial regulation that now led it to getting the license it needs to offer a predictions market.

"It's incredibly refreshing and invigorating to have a president and a financial regulator who are pro crypto, pro innovation, and pro America," said Gemini CEO Tyler Winklevoss in a news release. Major players in the cryptocurrency and investing sectors are rushing into prediction markets amid positive signals from Washington, D.C.

Robinhood Markets (HOOD) recently unveiled a series of predictions market and futures offerings. Brokerages such as Interactive Brokers (IBKR) have also introduced prediction-market products, while New York Stock Exchange owner Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) has taken a $2 billion stake in the largest predictions market, Polymarket.

Is GEMI Stock a Buy?

Gemini's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 11 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on six Buy and five Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average GEMI price target of $25.27 implies 79.09% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Gold Rallies to One-Month High Following Fed Rate Cut
data.description:
Gold futures are trading higher on Thursday and have reclaimed the $4,300 level after the Fed voted to cut interest rates by 25 bps. The precious metal is up by 4.7% over the past month and is currently trading at a one-month high.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Lower interest rates typically benefit gold because it doesn't pay out interest. Another way to put it is the opportunity cost of holding gold relative to interest-bearing assets, like Treasuries, goes down with lower rates.

Gold on Track for Best Year Since 1979

The Fed has lowered rates by a total of 75 bps this year in three separate Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings and is scheduled to meet again on January 28.

"Inflation hasn't really come back down to the Fed's 2% target, so, when you're lowering rates in an inflationary environment that is still not optimum, and that's very bullish for gold," said Marex analyst Edward Meir.

Spot gold (XAUUSD) has returned 60% year-to-date and is on track to cement its best year since 1979. The World Gold Council expects upside of 15% to 30% in 2026, driven by geopolitical risks, lower rates, and demand from exchange-traded funds (ETFs).

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Trump Faces Record Low Approval on Handling the Economy, Survey Shows
data.description:
31% of adults approve of President Trump's handling of the economy, marking a low for both his first and second term, according to a survey conducted by the Associated Press and the National Opinion Research Center (NORC). The survey includes responses from 1,146 adults between December 4 and 8.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Trump's economic management approval rating has steadily declined since reaching a second-term peak of 43% in August. Democrats and Republicans are sharply divided, with just 7% of Democrats approving compared with 69% of Republicans.

Unemployment and Sticky Inflation Highlight Economic Troubles

There are several indicators to gauge the health of the economy, such as the unemployment rate and inflation. As of September, the unemployment rate stood at 4.4%, a year-to-date high. In addition, inflation continues to be an issue for Americans and has stubbornly remained above the Fed's target of 2%.

The rating comes as several economists describe the economy as "K-shaped," which occurs when different areas of the economy experience different outcomes. The Fed's recent Beige Book report emphasized higher-income Americans driving spending, while low- and middle-income Americans are cutting back.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Rivian Stock (RIVN) Sinks after Revealing Its New Chip and Robotaxi Plans
data.description:
During its first "Autonomy and AI Day" in Palo Alto, EV maker Rivian Automotive (RIVN) announced that it built a custom chip, a new vehicle computer, and artificial intelligence models to bring self-driving features to its future cars. Starting in early 2026, Rivian plans to launch a subscription service called Autonomy+, which will offer autonomous driving capabilities powered by its new in-house processors and computer systems.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

The Autonomy+ service will be sold as a one-time $2,500 option or a monthly subscription starting at $49.99. For comparison, Tesla's (TSLA) premium Full Self-Driving (FSD) system costs $8,000 upfront or $99/month. In addition, Rivian says that an upcoming software update will include hands-free driving on over 3.5 million miles of marked roads across North America. Importantly, unlike Tesla, Rivian plans to use lidar and radar sensors to support Level 4 automation.

This would allow a car to drive itself under normal conditions while the passenger sleeps, similar to Waymo's robotaxis. Moreover, CEO RJ Scaringe said that the company will start with personally owned vehicles but aims to expand into robotaxis and ride-sharing in the future. This shift comes as EV sales slow down in the U.S. and global competition rises. And while the stock is up 25% this year, it's still down over 80% from its 2021 IPO and down over 7% at the time of writing.

Is RIVN Stock a Buy or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on RIVN stock based on five Buys, eight Holds, and six Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average RIVN price target of $13.87 per share implies 15.5% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
